findings.detail: Atomic write (tmp.write_text → tmp.replace) is internal change. External API save_json(subdir, filename, data) returns same Path. All callers (gate_archiver.py, gate_archiver tests) verified compatible. No caller depends on direct-write behavior.
ensure_dirs() iterates ('raw', 'analysis', 'decisions', 'review', 'gates'). Each uses mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True). Idempotent when 'gates' already exists. Safe for repeated calls.

save_json: dir_path.mkdir → tmp.write_text(JSON) → tmp.replace(filepath). If crash mid-write: .tmp partial but .replace never happens → original preserved. If crash after replace: .tmp already renamed → final file is complete. No partial-file corruption window.
If tmp.write_text() succeeds but tmp.replace() fails (e.g. directory-level disk full), .tmp orphaned. Not harmful but could accumulate. Minor.

notes: All checks pass. Atomic write pattern is correctly implemented. One minor note about orphaned .tmp on replace failure.
